thesis: Only U.S.-licensed HALEU producer with a $3.9B contracted backlog and $900M DOE investment. High short interest (21.6%) and 65% drawdown from highs create asymmetric risk/reward for risk-tolerant investors.

drivers: HALEU demand accelerates for SMRs and advanced reactors
DOE awards expand beyond $900M initial investment
SWU prices rise on supply tightness
Short squeeze on positive catalysts (21.6% short interest)

risks: Regulatory shifts reduce domestic enrichment urgency
High beta amplifies downside in risk-off markets

base_case.assumptions: Steady execution on backlog and guidance. HALEU production scales gradually 2026-2028. SWU prices remain firm. Stock recovers toward analyst consensus as execution proves out.

key_risks: Capital-intensive expansion strains free cash flow
Lumpy quarterly revenue due to contract timing
